issue_title: OCaml bindings for mk_fpa_max mk_fpa_min acting weird.
issue_body:
I use the following code to get the minimum of two numbers (in this case the same number twice).

```
let _ =
  try
    let ctx = Z3.mk_context [] in
    let ft = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_sort_32 ctx in 
    let t1 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_numeral_f ctx 0.5 ft in
    let t2 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_min ctx t1 t1 in
    let t3 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_eq ctx t1 t2 in 
    let solver = Z3.Solver.mk_simple_solver ctx in
    let result = Z3.Solver.check solver [t3] in
    let s = 
      match result with
      | Z3.Solver.SATISFIABLE -> "sat"
      | Z3.Solver.UNSATISFIABLE -> "unsat"
      | Z3.Solver.UNKNOWN -> "unknown"
    in
    Printf.printf "Test Z3: The check yields: %s\n" s
  with
  | Z3native.Exception exn -> Printf.printf "Test Z3: Z3native.Exception: %s\n" exn
  | _ -> Printf.printf "Test Z3: failed\n"
```
Unfortunately Z3 reports unknown for this code. This can't be right, can it?

Another weird message pops up using the same code to get the max of two (identical) numbers:
```
let _ =
  try
    let ctx = Z3.mk_context [] in
    let ft = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_sort_32 ctx in 
    let t1 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_numeral_f ctx 0.5 ft in
    let t2 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_max ctx t1 t1 in
    let t3 = Z3.FloatingPoint.mk_eq ctx t1 t2 in 
    let solver = Z3.Solver.mk_simple_solver ctx in
    let result = Z3.Solver.check solver [t3] in
    let s = 
      match result with
      | Z3.Solver.SATISFIABLE -> "sat"
      | Z3.Solver.UNSATISFIABLE -> "unsat"
      | Z3.Solver.UNKNOWN -> "unknown"
    in
    Printf.printf "Test Z3: The check yields: %s\n" s
  with
  | Z3native.Exception exn -> Printf.printf "Test Z3: Z3native.Exception: %s\n" exn
  | _ -> Printf.printf "Test Z3: failed\n"
```
Now Z3 throws an Exception "Z3native.Exception: invalid argument".
          
I have no clue why min and max behave differently in the first place. 
Next thing is why unknown or an Exception pops up. 
All other tests I ran seemed to be fine 
(I have written tests for almost all the floating point operations).

I am running Debian (Linux version 3.16.0-4-amd64) and Z3 4.4.2 
(downloaded and compiled today without problems). 
I also tried with Z3 4.4.0 and got the same output.

Any suggestions that might help to solve this are appreciated.

pyephem function is defined with next args:
```python
def pyephem(time, latitude, longitude, altitude=0, pressure=101325,
            temperature=12):

then we call it from get_solarposition.
```python
    elif method == 'pyephem':

                           **kwargs)

as far as altitude is missing and we are passing positional args -  pressure goes to altitude,  temperature goes to pressure and temperature receives its default value

issue_title: More efficient timegate query option
issue_body:
Great to see this library, I was just exploring using the `get_memento_info` for Netcapsule or Reconstruct and ran into the following issue.

While the `get_memento_info` is a great general purpose function, I think there needs to be a more optimized option that performs just the basic "Get memento from TimeGate at specified datetime" and nothing else. Ideally, this means having a single HTTP request to the TimeGate to get the desired info.

I propose adding an extra param, `include_uri_checks`, which will default to True, but when set to False, will disable any of the following additional checks.

Currently, the `get_memento_info` also includes the following:

* A `get_original_uri` queries the original url to determine if its a URL-R or URl-M. This is unnecessary if the user knows that a URL-R is being passed in (and potentially slow). With `include_uri_checks` disabled, this test will be skipped.

* Redirects are enabled on the TimeGate `HEAD` request, initiating potentially many redirects. This can be disabled to ensure just one request to the TimeGate. With `include_uri_checks` disabled,
the value of `Location` or `Content-Location` header will be used instead.

* Redirects are followed with a head request to the URL-M to get the `Memento-Datetime` header. This also brings additional overhead. Instead, the datetime is usually already available Link header response from the TimeGate, getting the `datetime` from the `rel=memento` entry. (As a side note, I was surprised to find that this is not required, though luckily present in most implementations -- seems like the timegate should always return the datetime in the link header, rather than force user to make another request to the URL-M)

* With this option, automatic redirecting, eg. `http://lanl.gov` -> `http://www.lanl.gov/worldview/` is disabled, as the users browser will follow the 302 explicitly and it will be reflected to the user.

For now, I've called this property `include_uri_checks` but can be changed to something else. If better to make a separate function, that can work as well.

With this option disabled, Reconstruct and Netcapsule can use start using this API, rather than relying on the existing Memento JSON api that's only on the aggregator.

I've also included a simple test file with demonstrates these changes.

Let me know if there are any questions/thoughts.

issue_title: Indicating that a function takes keyword-only arguments
issue_body:
## Issue description

I was told on gitter that pybind11 currently does not support creating functions that takes keyword-only arguments.  It would be appreciated if it did.

As far as I can tell, support for keyword-only arguments is directly implemented in PyArg_ParseTupleAndKeywords by the "$" specifier (https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/arg.html#other-objects) (which requires keyword-only arguments to have a default; I guess it's fine to have whatever limitations the Python C-API currently has).

On pybind11's side, I guess this can be provided as something like `py::kwonly("foo")` / `"foo"_kwo` where one would have expected `py::arg("foo")` / `"foo"_a`; or with some marker object separating kwonly arguments from non-kwonly ones, similar to `*` on Py3.

## Reproducible example code

N/A

issue_title: delete_blob fails if the blob has '.' in the name
issue_body:
It seems that delete_blob doesn't urlencode the name of the blob before sending to the REST API. If the name of the blob has a dot in it (.) the following call fails:

`block_blob_service.delete_blob('mycontainer', 'mydotted.blob')`

However, the following call succeeds:

`block_blob_service.delete_blob('mycontainer', urllib.quote('mydotted.blob'))`

Creating a blob with a dot in the name is not a problem though.

issue_title: SAS Key in Python SDK differs from C# by a leading '?'
issue_body:
`var sasToken = share.GetSharedAccessSignature(sharedAccessFilePolicy);` in c# will provide a sas token but with a '?' at the front.

The python equivalent, `sas_token = fs.generate_share_shared_access_signature(FILE_SHARE_NAME, expiry=expiry, permission=file.models.SharePermissions(read=True, write=True, delete=True, list=True))` does not.

The issue lies in that if you try to use the C# key, the python sdk fails. Simply trimming the leading '?' allows it to work.

issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**

We're using the Downloader class and it's showing erratic behavior in different scenarios:

- When downloading from a folder with only 1 file: Downloader class downloads only the file (e.g.: *sample_file.json*)
- When downloading from a folder with multiple files: Downloader class downloads the entire path structure until the folder being downloaded (e.g.: */path/to/folder/sample_file.json* and  */path/to/folder/sample_file2.json*)

Simply instantiate the class and let it download:

```python

    pex_builder = build_pex(reqs, options, resolver_options_builder)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/bin/pex.py", line 489, in build_pex
    resolveds = resolver.resolve(resolvables)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 200, in resolve
    dist = self.build(package, resolvable.options)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 257, in build
    dist = super(CachingResolver, self).build(package, options)
  File "/Users/saj/.pex/install/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl.1089a1bbad10037ad29421de8a162a77c90c8133/pex-1.1.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl/pex/resolver.py", line 168, in build
    raise Untranslateable('Package %s is not translateable by %s' % (package, translator))
pex.resolver.Untranslateable: Package SourcePackage(u'file:///Users/saj/.pex/build/backoff-1.1.0.tar.gz') is not translateable by ChainedTranslator(WheelTranslator, EggTranslator, SourceTranslator)
```

Even if we were to ignore the problem above, I don't think it is right to assume a UTF-8 encoding on all files.

From [PEP 263](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0263/):
> If a Unicode string with a coding declaration is passed to compile(), a SyntaxError will be raised.

From the same PEP:
> The builtin compile() API will be enhanced to accept Unicode as input. 8-bit string input is subject to the standard procedure for encoding detection as described above.

Instead of opening `setup.py` as a text file and decoding it ourselves, it sounds like it should be possible to hand a binary stream directly to the `compile()` builtin and let Python deal with this muck.

issue_title: Create context jars for javac invocations remotely when using the hermetic exec strategy
issue_body:
creating the `z.jar` files is part of the work for compiling java or scala sources. That work should also be happening in the hermetic context. With the implementation in #5999, the remote exec calls javac directly and  creating the context jar happens in jvm_compile at https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/602445ea77b5b7d31f8693bf905b074141c02052/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jvm_compile/jvm_compile.py#L704

Instead of doing that work there, we'd want it to be done by the binary that's invoked by the hermetic execution. That would require a shell script or something that bundles up the class files.

issue_title: Speedup aiohttp web server
issue_body:
The typical web handler looks like:
```
async def handler(request):
    ...  # do business logic
    return web.Response(text=..., content_type=...)

Unfortunately construction of `web.Response` is relative slow, `resp.prepare()` is even slower.
The code has too many checks for overlapping `content_type` with `headers['Content-Type']`, response length with `headers['Content-Length']`, HTTP chunk processing etc. etc.

We can speed up the situation if we assume that `web.Response` is constructed with well knows single text or binary data.

The suggestion is:
- Introduce `web.text_response()` and `web.bin_response()` like already present `web.json_response()`. Functions will just make `web.Response` instances on this first step. The main point is teaching people to use the new API.
- `web.Response` should be kept as is for backward compatibility.
- After that we can refactor our response classes hierarchy by making a `BaseResponse` as parent for `StreamResponse` and deriving private classes for binary and text responses from `BaseResponse`. Obviously `BaseResponse.prepare()` should exist, not sure about other API attributes.
- These private classes can do as minimal job as possible, they don't need to pass redundant checks for conflicting HTTP headers.

We should forbid the following HTTP headers in `web.text_response()` / `web.bin_response()`:
-  Connection
-  Keep-Alive
-  Trailer
-  Transfer-Encoding
-  Content-Length
-  Upgrade

The check for headers is quite expensive, let's do it in debug mode only (`loop.get_debug() is True` or `PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG=1`.

That's it.

Discussion is welcome.
PR for the first step (adding `web.text_response()` and `web.bin_response()`) is even more welcome.

issue_title: _RequestContextManager will not close connection if ClientConnectorError has happened
issue_body:
## Long story short

When using _RequestContextManager and try to get a host which can not be connected to, connection will not be released to the pool.

## Expected behaviour

Raises an Exception and connection releases.

## Actual behaviour

Raises an Exception.

## Steps to reproduce

```
import asyncio

import aiohttp


async def get_image(session, url):
    async with session.get(url) as response:
        if response.status != 200:
            print('Something went wrong!')
            return None

        return await response.read()


async def worker():
    conn = aiohttp.TCPConnector(limit=1)
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=conn) as session:
        await asyncio.gather(
            asyncio.ensure_future(get_image(session, 'http://unavailable:9090/test.jpg')),
            asyncio.ensure_future(get_image(session, 'http://unavailable:9090/test.jpg'))
        )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

    loop.run_until_complete(worker())
    loop.close()
```

## Your environment

macOS 10.12.6
Python 3.6.1
aiohttp 2.2.5

issue_title: [client] Using connection pool without session or session without cookies
issue_body:
## Long story short

Sometimes one needs to make requests to a number of external http resources, some of them being client-facing API providing cookies you do not really need. The Session manages cookies by default and there's no simple way to easily switch it off. In fact, in many situations only connection pooling is required. Creating a session for every request does not look as a good approach.

A typical use case is a BFF, orchestrating multiple microservices.

## Expected behaviour

1) `aiohttp.ClientSession(disable_cookies=True)`
2) `aiohttp.ClientConnPool()` and then just `pool.get()` with same syntax as session.

If there is a simple way to achieve one of these, an example in the docs would be awesome.

Thanks in advace!

issue_title: AppVeyor create invalid wheel file names for iterative builds
issue_body:
The recently merged PR https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/857 allowed for iterative NuPIC builds to pick up a CORE_COMMITISH SHA labelled nupic.bindings wheel from AWS S3. The addition of the long-SHA into the wheel file name worked in that instance, but has subsequently failed for certain NuPIC AppVeyor builds [1].

The invalid Pip wheel file name error is thrown via this [2] InvalidWheelFilename assert. Pip wheel.py uses a regex to split a wheel file name string into certain variables. With the `ver` regex capture point (`(?P<ver>\d.*?)`) assuming that wheel version strings in the file name start with a digit (`\d.*`).

The AppVeyor yaml script requires a change to make sure that the `ver` part of iterative build wheels starts with a digit before the long-SHA. Proposal is to prepend the BINDINGS_VERSION to the long-SHA here [3], and the wheels subsequent upload to S3 here [4].

An online regex editor, such as https://regex101.com/#python, can be used to validate this required change.

1 https://ci.appveyor.com/project/numenta-ci/nupic/build/0.1.0.1204
2 https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/develop/pip/wheel.py#L616
3 https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/appveyor.yml#L129
4 https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/appveyor.yml#L162

class Foo(Base):

issue_title: Immutable diffs
issue_body:
Current implementation returns diffs that reference the original structure. Thus the diff may change later when the structures change.

This is counter to at least my intuitive expectation that the diff is a snapshot of the structures at the time of running the diff.

I would request that the diff returns (deep) copies of the referenced structures by default, with maybe an option for returning references instead.

At the very least I recommend making this very explicit in the documentation - I just suffered from some very hard to debug bugs because of this.

Thanks!

issue_title: Pipenv workflow for private authenticated package indexes like Artifactory
issue_body:
Hi there, I'm looking to switch from plain old pip to pipenv but I'm having trouble finding a comparable workflow for private package indexes that require HTTP Basic Auth specified in the index URL.

With plain old pip (9.0.1), it's quite easy to point to a package index with basic auth using `~/.pip/pip.conf` or `$VIRTUAL_ENV/pip.conf`:
```
[global]
index-url = https://MY_USERNAME:MY_PASSWORD@some.artifactory.mycorp.com/artifactory/api/pypi/python/simple
```

Because of the basic auth in the URL, it's actually beneficial that this is kept separate from our dependencies. We track our requirements in git; meanwhile the creds are kept in an untracked config file that varies for each member of our team (since they'll all have their own personal creds).

Can pipenv support a workflow like this?

FWIW I tried removing the `[[sources]]` section in hopes that it would fall back to `pip.conf`, but no such luck. At least for us, this would be quite amenable as every package is expected to be pulled from our single private index anyway.

Thanks!

##### Describe your environment

1. OSX 10.12
1. Python version: 3.6.2
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.3

issue_title: deprecation warning with Cython build_ext prints
issue_body:
When Cython is installed, the setuptools `build_ext` command tries to use `Cython.Distutils.build_ext` to build extension modules:

https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/command/build_ext.py#L17

With Cython 0.25, the distutils extension `Cython.Distutils.build_ext` was updated to use `cythonize` (which properly handles dependencies), and the old distutils extension deprecated and moved to `Cython.Distutils.old_build_ext`.

The change was temporarily reverted with Cython 0.25.1 to give projects time to migrate, but a deprecation warning is still printed when using Cython's `old_build_ext` as setuptools does.

```
...lib/python3.5/site-packages/Cython/Distutils/old_build_ext.py:30: UserWarning: Cython.Distutils.old_build_ext does not properly handle dependencies and is deprecated.
  "Cython.Distutils.old_build_ext does not properly handle dependencies "
```

I assume `setuptools.commands.build_ext` will have to be updated to import the `new_build_ext` from `Cython.Distutils.build_ext`.

issue_title: Add ability to swallow I/O errors
issue_body:
When monitoring streamparse topologies with tools like Sentry, receiving tons of errors like:

```
Received EOF while trying to read stdin from Storm, pipe appears to be broken, exiting.
Error streamparse.storm.component in read_messagestreamparse.ipc
```

Lead to alerting fatigue (we've had 5.9K of these in just one of our production topologies, as @dan-blanchard knows :)). The IPC layer shutting down like this, is usually because of another issue like some other component dying and Storm killing components or rebalancing a topology. In the former case, the actual exception is captured elsewhere already, in the latter, it's a non-error. Either way, the utility of the IPC exception is questionable.

My gut instinct is just swallow these exceptions full stop, but maybe others have use for them. Ideas on how to handle the ability to stop these from being raised?

issue_title: "workers" key in config.json does not seem to be working
Refactor unique_fields to reflect named URL structure
issue_body:
In tower-cli we have a variable called `unique_fields`, but all of its actual use has been removed by now, and at this point it just does extra computation in the metaclass.

Additionally, the construct itself is fundamentally incorrect / outdated. Uniqueness is most often dictated by another related resource, and that related resource may, itself, have another related resource that dictates its uniqueness. The fundamentals were laid out in a fully correct manner for the AWX named URL feature:

https://github.com/ansible/awx/blob/devel/docs/named_url.md

This card is to remove the current pointless use of `unique_fields` and introduce some other mechanism to track uniqueness, such that we can correctly determine a uniqueness tuple, even crossing over multiple related items.

This may not be immediately useful in a functional sense, but having the data available and correct on the `Resource` classes would open up future refining of related lookups and uniqueness exception handling.

issue_title: Add commands for Transfer endpoint manager APIs
issue_body:
We're working through the last of endpoint manager / "management console" ( https://github.com/globus/globus-sdk-python/milestone/11 ) support in the SDK now.

It would be nice to have a CLI version with commands for all of these activities.
Command structure/names TBD.

`globus admin` or `globus endpoint admin` have a good feel, as they connote the user->endpoint or user->task relationship.
i.e. `globus admin task pause` and `globus admin task cancel` "make sense"

We should also consider `globus endpoint manager`, `globus manage`, and the related families of command names.

Having the activities delineated like the rest of the CLI seems good for consistency. That means using the operative entity like `endpoint`, `task`, `bookmark`, and `endpoint server` as an intermediate command name, and the verb, `show`, `create`, `delete`, as a leaf command.
So, `globus admin task pause` is good, but `globus admin pause task` is bad.

Currently blocked on upcoming SDK release.

issue_title: Data augmentation modifies the original input data(?)
issue_body:
When processing an input mini batch of images with data augmentation (e.g., random blur) during training, are the original images also modified? Normally, I would expect that a copy of each batch is processed at each iteration and the original images are not modified, otherwise, the transformations are accumulated and this is not expected.

Is this the case in tflearn? If it is, then it is very important and should at least be emphasized in the documentation.

I have been doing cross-validation and realized that the validation performance steadily drops at each cross-validation iteration, which was unexpected. Then, I provided the training with a new copy of the data at each CV iteration and the results improved significantly. However this is only a partial solution, if the original training data is overwritten/modified by data augmentation, since it gets accumulated at each epoch.

issue_title: Smoothed curves have a kink in them
issue_body:
After upgrading to Tensorboard 1.0.0a6 (from PyPI), I started seeing kinks in smoothed graphs. These give the impression that the value suddenly changed at a much faster rate, but in fact the kinks are an artefact of the smoothing algorithm; the kink moves with the smoothing factor:

![smoothing-900-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542059-c4d12408-c471-11e7-9296-9f98bab5ef03.png)
![smoothing-925-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542062-c4e6d2da-c471-11e7-9638-51f25b8dcb15.png)
![smoothing-95-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542058-c4bd8218-c471-11e7-83f0-02eee5a237e5.png)

From top to bottom, a smoothing factor of 0.9, 0.925, and 0.95.

I did not see those kinks with the version I used before. Unfortunately I have no record of which version I was using before 1.0.0a6. In that version the tail of the curve was also smoothed, unlike in 1.0.0.0a6 where the last smoothed point coincides with the last data point.

issue_title: Safari 404: web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map & weblas.map.json
issue_body:
## Safari bug with TensorBoard server
### Path not found (404): web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map and weblas.map.json
Safari browser access error with TensorBoard server, after building the TensorBoard github source on a standard Debian VM. This bug has been described many times before and I've tried various fixes unsuccessfully, so it may be something peculiar with my Safari as there is no problem with Chrome/Firefox.

I simplified the setup so the conditions can be recreated independently. My hope is that someone can confirm whether TensorBoard is accessible on their Safari with the below setup.

### System config:
Google Cloud Platform - Compute Engine - VM Instance
Standard 1 vCPU
Debian GNU/Linux 9 (stretch)
Firewall: Allow HTTP/HTTPS traffic
Network: tcp:6010 allow

### Install Git, Bazel, pip and tensorflow:
```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y git openjdk-8-jdk python-pip
echo "deb [arch=amd64] http://storage.googleapis.com/bazel-apt stable jdk1.8" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/bazel.list
curl https://bazel.build/bazel-release.pub.gpg | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y bazel
sudo apt-get upgrade -y bazel
pip install tensorflow
```

### Clone TensorBoard and Bazel run server:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard.git
cd tensorboard
bazel run tensorboard -- --logdir /home/emnist-2000 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 6010
```

### Server output:
When accessing the server with client-side Safari browser, the following server messages appear:
```
TensorBoard 0.4.0a0 at http://0.0.0.0:6010 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
W1104 15:20:57.933438 Thread-2 application.py:268] path /web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map not found, sending 404
W1104 15:20:58.725778 Thread-3 application.py:268] path /weblas.map.json not found, sending 404
```

### Safari error:
Safari (Version 10.0.3 (12602.4.8)) on macOS Sierra (10.12.3), several soft and hard reloads, empty cache, disable cache, restarted.
```
/web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map - Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (NOT FOUND)
/weblas.map.json - Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (NOT FOUND)
```

Dataset for tensorboard: [emnist-2000.zip](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/files/1443323/emnist-2000.zip)

issue_title: In-app notifications
issue_body:
Since we can't rely on the HTML5 Notifications API for feedback, on e.g. publishing, we should create an in-app notifications section. I'd be happy with the [Atom style notifications](https://github.com/atom/notifications), even if we use that package directly:

![Notification Stack](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/69169/5176406/350d0e80-73fd-11e4-8101-1776b9d6d8bf.gif)

For the sake of the publishing workflow, we want the notification box to have an `onclick` (that one can set to open a remote URL).

issue_title: Maintenance Wishlist
issue_body:
Hey all, there have been some old parts of desktop that I'd love to revisit. I keep thinking about them as I touch various parts of the app. It's about time I outline what some of them are, either to document what I'm going to go after or see if others want to tackle some. Without further ado:

* [ ] Collapse all the reducers into one that are practically the same `setIn` operation on a cell with a different path. All the action creators will end up creating one kind of action for reduction, and it will simplify any additions to metadata changing.
* [ ] Merge the `metadata` reducer into the document reducer (which will involve changing the usage)
* [ ] Delete the constants.js file, which to literals. Since we're using flow, we can use the literal type names.
* [ ] Unify all the action flow types. We've duplicated them across `actions.js` and the reducers

https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/commit/7cde41ea934f43e66dc123ea6411d8535b50a4f7

Reiterating my comment - if devs want to use less or scss files, that should be their perogative, but the default setup for FA's bower.json should be pure CSS and not assume any processing will take place.

If people want to use SCSS or LESS and compile some customizations into FA, then overrides are appropriate. With this new bower.json, overrides will be *required* for the vast majority of FA users.

issue_title: Action option allow_error
issue_body:
Sometimes some script or command will return non-zero status but we do not want such errors to interrupt the whole process. One way to do this is to

```
try:
    run('''script''')
except:
   pass
```

but this makes the script less easy to read.

Another method is to 

```
run:
    cmd_that_might_fail || true
```

and makes the command always return true, but this method is not portable.

An option `allow_error` would be nice to translate all errors from actions to warning.

issue_title: Calling magics from sub kernels.
issue_body:
I am not quite sure how to execute magics from subkernels but we should perhaps allow passing magics to subkernels if we are to keep maximum compatibility with them. Then we should adjust our magic syntax to accommodate this. For example, right now

```
%magic1

%magic2
statement
```
are all sos magics, perhaps we should require that sos magics to start from the first line and without newline, and other magics should be separated form sos magics by an empty newline. That is to say, in the above example, we can treat `%magic1` as sos magic, and `%magic2` as subkernel magic.

Of course we will need to first make sure we could call magics of subkernels.

Issues running Unity wrapper (High CPU usage/No texture write)
issue_body:
* Before opening a new issue, we wanted to provide you with some useful suggestions (Click "Preview" above for a better view):

issue_title: The documentation is promising a feature that is not there yet
issue_body:
The documentation states:

> As a convenience, layers are also available directly on the connection object. The above expressions are equivalent to the following:
> 
> ```

issue_title: Exceedingly slow response time for HTTP requests
issue_body:
I'm running a very simple (backend-wise) app with Daphne 2.0 as the app server. The app is hosted on Heroku. I recently noticed that HTTP requests on the live webpage are _really_ slow when using Daphne.

I ran some speed tests for Daphne vs. Gunicorn. The web worker run command was the only thing I changed between tests.

My `routing.py` file:

``` python
application = ProtocolTypeRouter({
    'websocket': AuthMiddlewareStack(
        URLRouter([
            url('^chat/stream/$', ChatbotConsumer),
        ]),
    ),
})
```

However, keep in mind that I never triggered the `/chat/stream/` endpoint when running these tests, so requests were all HTTP. As I [understand it](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/topics/routing.html#protocoltyperouter), leaving `'http'` unrouted defaults to using Django's usual method of handing HTTP requests.

---

For a web worker using `daphne myproject.asgi:application -b 0.0.0.0 -p $PORT`:

![daphne waterfall](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6445661/37887487-aa07599e-3087-11e8-9807-da2d98e35e83.png)

For the exact same app, using `gunicorn myproject.wsgi`:

![gunicorn waterfall](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6445661/37887493-b689f73a-3087-11e8-84a2-f56efbccb19d.png)

Notice the extreme disparity between the two: 2000 ms vs. 100 ms for small static files. The call to `/app-data` simply loads user-based information using the Django ORM.

---

Some Heroku metrics, tested by clearing the cache and refreshing the page very five minutes. Daphne is on the left and right, Gunicorn in the middle.

![selection_014](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6445661/37887598-4c67f162-3088-11e8-81d3-56d7fe732a8c.png)

![selection_015](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6445661/37887619-709252b2-3088-11e8-8ee6-a7cc9afa4f1b.png)

![selection_013](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6445661/37887657-9ec9df42-3088-11e8-9311-adfd98860384.png)

I couldn't definitively determine if the app was also more likely to have a high Dyno Load when using Daphne, but that seemed to be the case. There's no memory issue; memory usage was slightly lower for Daphne than Gunicorn.

---

Any ideas?

issue_title: Definition and accuracy of reflectance
issue_body:
While trying to get reflectances from MSG SEVIRI solar channel HRIT files using satpy I encountered several problems.

First of all, for calibration=='reflectance' you do not actually get reflectance according to the usual definition, but reflectance * cos(SZA) * d^-2, where SZA is the solar zenith angle and d is the sun-earth distance in astronomical units. Nevertheless the standard_name is set to "toa_bidirectional_reflectance" -- this is confusing. Moreover, reflectance * cos(SZA) * d^-2 is not really a useful quantity...

It would be great if satpy could calculate real reflectances and it would be even better if the accuracy of these reflectances was sufficiently high for applications like data assimilation. 

There is some code to account for cos(SZA) in the composite module. However, this code computes SZA for a fixed time, the "start_time". Ignoring the variation of the solar zenith angle during image acquisition can cause large errors when the sun is low and causes errors of a few percent in the northern hemisphere even during the day (SEVIRI scans from south to north, so start_time is wrong by about 11 minutes in Europe). For this reason I am computing cos(SZA) like this:
```
mu0 = np.ma.zeros((ny,nx))
Tacq = gs.datasets[chan].info["end_time"] - gs.datasets[chan].info["start_time"]
for j in range(ny) :
    tacq = gs.datasets[chan].info["start_time"] + timedelta( seconds=(j/float(ny))*Tacq.seconds )
    mu0[j,:] = np.ma.masked_outside( cos_zen( taqu, lon2d[j,:], lat2d[j,:]),0.035, 1, copy=False)
```

Ignoring variations in the sun-earth distance can cause reflectance errors of up to +-3%.
To account for the variation I am now using
`reflectance *= float(sunpy.sun.sunearth_distance( t=time_slot ) / astropy.units.AU)**2`
Maybe sunearth_distance could be integrated in pyorbital?

Only with these two corrections I get reflectances that are compatible (error<1%) with what I got using other satellite software packages.

issue_title: Allow providing effective wavelength, thus bypassing the RSR data
issue_body:
#### Problem description

The atmospheric correction in the visible range currently requires access to the satellite sensor RSR functions. Many sensors are already available in PySPectral. However, it would be nice/helpful if one could derive the Rayleigh correction without having the RSR implemented in PySpectral. It would just require the user to provide a central or rayleigh scattering effective wavelength, supposing the user could calculate this herself.

#### Code example
Potentially the code could look like this:

```
from pyspectral.rayleigh import Rayleigh
viirs = Rayleigh('Suomi-NPP', 'viirs')
corr = viirs.get_reflectance(sun_zenith, sat_zenith, azidiff, effective_wavelength=0.486, refl_m05)
```
Currently the last call as to llok like this:
```
corr = viirs.get_reflectance(sun_zenith, sat_zenith, azidiff, 'M03', refl_m05)
```

Thank you for reporting an issue !

issue_title: morphology.local_maxima errors when no maxima present
issue_body:
## Description
When invoking morphology.local_maxima with an array where all values are equal, it should return zeros_like(image). Instead, it errors. The error is in _find_min_diff:

img_vec = np.unique(image.flatten())

issue_title: codecov --help fails out of Git repo
issue_body:
Running `codecov --help` outside of a Git repo displays a traceback:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/vv/bin/codecov", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('codecov==1.1.9', 'console_scripts', 'codecov')()
  File "/tmp/vv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/codecov/__init__.py", line 286, in cli
    data, codecov = main()
  File "/tmp/vv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/codecov/__init__.py", line 259, in main
    branch = subprocess.check_output('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', shell=True)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/subprocess.py", line 573, in check_output
    raise CalledProcessError(retcode, cmd, output=output)
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' returned non-zero exit status 128
```
This is because the check `'-h' not in argv and '--help' not in argv` only works if `argv` is set; else it should check sys.argv (this is what argparse does line 276).

I personally recommend something like `if not argv: argv = sys.argv` at the beginning of main() to avoid unnecessary ifs.

issue_title: Make stacked environments configurable
issue_body:
When conda is activated, the `bin` directory of the `root` environment is in the `PATH`.
When another environment is activated, its `bin` directory is prepended to the `PATH`, however, the root's `bin` is still in the `PATH` as the second item.
It leads to the situations like the following.

I have `root` environment where `ipython` is installed, for example.
Then I create a new environment, say, `test` and install some package there, which is not installed in my root environment, for example, `clawpack`. However, I forget to install `ipython` in the `test` environment.

Then I activate my `test` environment, execute `ipython` and type `import clawpack`.
What I get is an import error, because `ipython` that I use comes from the `root` environment, not from the `test` environment.

A similar situation occurs when I install into the `test` environment a different version of a package.

It seems to me that the `bin` of the `root` environment should be removed from `PATH` when another environment is activated. Then, during deactivation, `root` environment should be pushed back to the `PATH`.

issue_title: Consider removing link and unlink locks in lieu of undefined concurrent behavior
issue_body:
Locks are problematic in many ways.  There are multiple issues filed about them.  Yet the also ensure safety, in so far as it confines the scope of and reasoning about the code.

For 4.3, at least for a first canary release, I'm open to experimenting with removing locks for link and unlink operations.  The *strict* limitation will be that running link/unlink operations currently (i.e. in two open terminals) against the same prefix will lead to likely broken and certainly undefined behavior.

issue_title: offline install, overriding channels
issue_body:
I have issues with `conda install --offline --override-channels -c file://<my-channel-url>`

```python
RuntimeError: EnforceUnusedAdapter called with url https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch/repodata.json.bz2
This command is using a remote connection in offline mode
```

When overriding channels in offline mode, why is https://repo.continuum.io used when fetching repo data ?
Currently, a simple way to avoid this error is to patch **conda/core/repodata.py**:

```python
def fetch_repodata_remote_request(session, url, etag, mod_stamp):
    # <...>
    if 'repo.continuum.io' in url or url.startswith("file://"):
        filename = 'repodata.json.bz2'
        headers['Accept-Encoding'] = 'identity'
    else:
        headers['Accept-Encoding'] = 'gzip, deflate, compress, identity'
        headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
        filename = 'repodata.json'

    # prevents offline error                                      <--- addition
    if 'repo.continuum.io' in url and context.offline:          # <--- addition
        return None                                             # <--- addition
```

Is this expected, and is there a better solution ?

issue_title: "No such file or directory" during multiple parallel activations
issue_body:
Nearly identically to #2837, I am also working in a clustered environment where multiple parallel processes are activating the same env simultaneously and I am getting "No such file or directory" regularly.

In fact running the following script, in 3 different terminals at once, will reproduce the problem within a few seconds:

```
#!/bin/bash
# Look for race condition in activating an anaconda env
# Run this in multiple windows at once and wait for one of them to crash.

ret=0
while [ "$ret" == "0" ]; do
    echo -n .
    source activate myenv
    ret=$?
done
```

The immediate solution (as #2837 did) would appear to be to add another exception to the exception [in install.py in symlink_conda_hlp at ~line 616](https://github.com/kalefranz/conda/blob/8b5bc4ec76075f2896d5a53d1b5e41a0194a9c11/conda/install.py#L616).  This time adding errno.ENOENT to the list.

So many exceptions to exceptions raises the question in my mind if re-symlinking is the right thing to do during activation.   If the symlinks can't be entirely removed, could this be replaced with just a check if the symlinks are there and a warning if somehow they've gone missing?  Keeping it read-only will avoid these race conditions.  (In fact, the above suggestion probably doesn't remove all the possible race conditions here.)  An arg to activate to force re-symlinking could be added to override the default of not re-symlinking.  Just some thoughts...

CC'ing NERSC admins who have helped me debug this: @rcthomas

issue_title: Start using _conda_info as the directory for meta-data
issue_body: Using info as the directory for meta-data is not forward-looking as it will conflict with any package that uses info.  Start using _conda_info as the directory for meta-data (leaving info as backward compatibility).

issue_title: Drop state chain?
issue_body: The [`aspen.request_processor.algorithm` module](https://github.com/AspenWeb/aspen.py/blob/380ecf37069af939315115c42dc8c314e0920ac3/aspen/request_processor/algorithm.py) only contains 7 functions, and they're all very simple. I'm not sure using a state chain in Aspen still makes sense, framework wrappers may be better off gluing the pieces together themselves.

issue_title: Add support for different query language dialects
issue_body: Add support for setting a query language dialect so that pypika can generate database specific SQL.  Include dialects for MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, and Vertica

issue_title: Support analytic queries for Vertica and Oracle
issue_body:
https://my.vertica.com/docs/7.1.x/HTML/Content/Authoring/SQLReferenceManual/Functions/Analytic/AnalyticFunctions.htm

Add support for analytical functions and queries using PARTITION OVER

issue_title: Descriptions derived from docstrings appear as code blocks in GraphiQL
issue_body:
This is because when a description is written with indentation, e.g.

```
class X:
    """
    The docstring of class X
    """
```

the description is `"\n    The docstring of class X\n    "`

issue_title: AbstractType is shown in documentation, but raises Deprecation warning
issue_body:
The [django/relay documentation](http://docs.graphene-python.org/projects/django/en/latest/tutorial-relay/) shows the use of `AbstractType`, but this raises a DeprecationWarning at runtime.

Additionally, the [URL provided](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene/blob/2.0/UPGRADE-v2.0.md#deprecations) in the DeprecationWarning message returns a 404

issue_title: Annotated field not resolving into type field
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a query class with a custom resolver it returns:
`Foo.object.all().extra(select={"date": "strftime('%%Y-%%m-%%d %%H:%%M',datetime)"}).values('date', 'name').annotate(avg_value=Avg('value', output_field=FloatField())).order_by('date')`

In my type I added the following field:
`avg_value = graphene.Float(description='The avg value connected to that Foo')`

When I log at the end of the resolver it has an average. When i recieve the data in my frontend it is gone. Maybe I'am doing something wrong but there are no examples to be found about annotated fields.

issue_title: tensorlayer output
issue_body: Is there a function to return the probability of the outputs? I searched utils.py and couldn't find what I wanted.

issue_title: Add --traceback option
issue_body: When traceback is enabled, PavementErrors are not hidden, but propagated further.

issue_title: Allow linking to Doxygen groups
issue_body: Groups in Doxygen can be created with `\defgroup`. Doxylink should allow linking directly to them.

issue_title: bank.withdraw/deposit_credits breaks mongodb with floats
issue_body:
# Other bugs

If you give bank.deposit_credits or bank.withdraw_credit a float instead of an int while using MongoDB, the users balance is stuck as a double. Because of this many cogs break because they expect an int, but get a float.

#### What were you trying to do?

I was trying to withdraw credits from a users account with

`await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)`

although due to a bug I accidentaly ran

```
await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
await bank.deposit_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
````

This is most likely unrelated to the bug, but I thought I should mention it anyway.

#### What were you expecting to happen?

I expected red to either throw an error or convert the float to an int 

#### What actually happened?

Red carried on, as if the value was an int. It changed the value type of the balance in mongodb from an int32 to a double. This breaks many other cogs because they expect an int but get a double.

#### How can we reproduce this issue?

1. run the following command

```
await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
await bank.deposit_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
```

2. done

issue_title: Copying Headers in OpenThread
issue_body:
I noticed that when the Ip6 layer processes a received packet, it first copies the header out of the message to the stack, and then processes the header. Similarly, when sending a packet, it first constructs the header on the stack, and then copies it into the Message. Other layer of the protocol stack (e.g., UDP) work in the same way.

However, if OPENTHREAD_CONFIG_MESSAGE_BUFFER_SIZE is tuned correctly, then all of the headers are likely to be in the first buffer in the Message. The headers won't be split across multiple Message buffers. Therefore, it would be more efficient to just process headers by reading the memory in the Message buffer itself, instead of first copying it to a stack buffer.

This could be accomplished by adding a public method to the Message class:

```
uint8_t* GetPointer(uint16_t aOffset) const;
```

And, of course, one would modify the IP6 and UDP layers to just construct headers (for sending packets) and process headers (for receiving packets) directly in Messages. This would be more efficient than copying messages to the stack.

I would be happy to contribute this fix if the developers are receptive to this idea.

issue_title: cc2538 FLASH settings location
issue_body:
Probably for some historic reason, the location of the CC2538 FLASH settings is in the middle of the flash at address 0x00203900 - which if you turn on features, and turn the logs up the flash grows and then the two areas (code/text) vrs (settings) overlap and things go horribly wrong.

Most typically the initialized data goes bad at startup because the ".data" initializes are are stored at the END of the .text section, and thus these are the first to get wiped out, or partially wiped out depending upon how they align verses the page boundary. 

We have a fix for this but are not sure about the origins of the "0x39000" magic number in settings.cpp

Basically we move the SETTINGS storage to the *LAST* two pages  *END* of physical flash, just before the "CCA"  (customer configuration area) where FACTORY settings are normally stored)
thus the last 3 page are set aside as follows:

(N-1) = CCA area, length = 2k
(N-2) = Thread Settings Storage Area (B)
(N-3) = Thread Settings Storage Area (A)

issue_title: stdout capture on Windows works no more (sacred0.7b3)
issue_body:
I am unable to see my experiments output on Windows.

E.g. a script like the attached one prints nothing to the standard output and no output is stored into a database until the experiment finishes.

I managed to make it work with the latest 0.6.x version, but with none of the 0.7bx :(

```python
from sacred import Experiment
import time
import random
ex = Experiment("Fake experiment")

@ex.config
def cfg():
   parameter1 = 1
   parameter2 = "some value"
   complex_parameter = {"par1": "val1", "clock": time.clock()}

@ex.automain
def my_main(_run, complex_parameter):
	counter = 0
	print("Running experiment. par1=%s" % complex_parameter["par1"])
	while True:
		if counter == 20:
			break
		counter+=1
		print("Update #%d" % counter)
		ms_to_wait = random.randint(5, 5000)
		time.sleep(ms_to_wait/1000)

	return 100.50
```

issue_title: How to update an IntSlider from "inside" the function decorated by interact()?
issue_body:
I want to visualize a signal's channel (`signal` is has dimension (n_samples, n_channels)) using interact, but before retrieving it I don't know how many channels it will have. Is there a way I can dynamically update the channel slider max value with the `n_channels` information?
```
def foo(index, channel):
    signal = get_signal(S, index)
    n_channels = signal.shape[1]
    plt.plot(signal[:,channel])
    plt.show()
interact(foo, index=(0,n_sessions-1), channel=(1,10))
```
I tried defining an enclosed function like:
```
def foo(index):
    def foo2(channel):
         plt.plot(signal[:,channel])
         plt.show()
    signal = get_signal(S, index)
    n_channels = signal.shape[1]
    interact(foo2, channel=(0,n_channels-1))
interact(foo, index=(0,n_sessions-1))
```
But this way, when I change the index slider, it creates a new interact output below the old one (I tried using close() and clear_output() but it didn't work)
I've also tried using ipywidgets `observe` but couldn't work it out.

WITH python wheel installed:
```
(mozilla_ds) ➜  mozilla_ds git:(master) ✗ ./bin/run-ldc93s1.sh

It seems like the key line is:
`libc++abi.dylib: terminating with uncaught exception of type std::__1::system_error: mutex lock failed: Invalid argument`

Is this a problem with the `libctc_decoder_with_kenlm.so`? Any help would be appreciated; I've been stuck on this for several days.

issue_title: All TaskCluster builds (DeepSpeech, Tensorflow) are broken
issue_body: This is fallout from https://github.com/libgit2/libgit2/issues/4343#issuecomment-328631745, also reported against tensorflow: https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/12979

issue_title: Compare DS2 implementations
issue_body: Would be interesting to compare our implementation of DS2 with [Nervana's](https://github.com/NervanaSystems/deepspeech) and [Baidu's](https://github.com/baidu-research/ba-dls-deepspeech).

issue_title: Wrong build number displayed for candidate build
issue_body:
Just run the following command to see the broken output. Luckily we still download the correct version.

```
% mozdownload -t candidate -v 53.0
INFO | Retrieving list of candidate builds from https://archive.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/candidates/53.0-candidates/
INFO | Found 4 builds: build1, build3, build5, build6
INFO | Selected build: build4
INFO | Downloading from: https://archive.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/candidates/53.0-candidates/build6/mac/en-US/Firefox%2053.0.dmg
INFO | Saving as: /Volumes/data/code/mozdownload/firefox-53.0-build6.en-US.mac.dmg
```

I assume that we print the index of the build but not the actual name what we should do.

issue_title: columns is 0 when redirecting output
issue_body:
Looks like [`columns`](https://github.com/cs50/submit50/blob/1e122e51105ef02eff8d2de4db6939e538942175/submit50#L243) is `0` if you redirect output, which causes an exception per https://travis-ci.org/cs50/submit50/jobs/238360420#L479

```
$ submit50 --help > foo
Error in sys.excepthook:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 266, in excepthook
    cprint("Sorry, something's wrong! Let sysadmins@cs50.harvard.edu know!", "yellow")
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 254, in cprint
    termcolor.cprint(textwrap.fill(text, columns), color=color, on_color=on_color, attrs=attrs, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 377, in fill
    return w.fill(text)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 349, in fill
    return "\n".join(self.wrap(text))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 340, in wrap
    return self._wrap_chunks(chunks)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 234, in _wrap_chunks
    raise ValueError("invalid width %r (must be > 0)" % self.width)
ValueError: invalid width 0 (must be > 0)

Original exception was:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/kareem/.virtualenvs/submit50/bin/submit50", line 6, in <module>
    exec(compile(open(__file__).read(), __file__, 'exec'))
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 524, in <module>
    main()
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 90, in main
    usage()
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 485, in usage
    cprint("Usage: submit50 problem")
  File "/home/kareem/github/cs50/submit50/submit50", line 254, in cprint
    termcolor.cprint(textwrap.fill(text, columns), color=color, on_color=on_color, attrs=attrs, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 377, in fill
    return w.fill(text)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 349, in fill
    return "\n".join(self.wrap(text))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 340, in wrap
    return self._wrap_chunks(chunks)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/textwrap.py", line 234, in _wrap_chunks
    raise ValueError("invalid width %r (must be > 0)" % self.width)
ValueError: invalid width 0 (must be > 0)
```

Perhaps we shouldn't rely on `get_terminal_size`'s fallback value and just hard-code `column or 80`?

issue_title: pyserial timeout not raising an exception
issue_body:
Hello,
I've seen that, when setting a timeout, read_until() calls (or other read() calls) on serial objects don't raise an exception if timeout occurs; they simply return, with no way for the caller to understand whether the read succeeded (until the terminator) or not. Or, if there's any, I couldn't tell.

This is rather different from common nonblocking file handling behaviour (where an exception is raised if no data is available for such timeout) or at least there's a way to understand if there's data somewhere (e.g. the select() call).

Was this a design choice? Would you be interested in a contributed patch with some backward-compatible option that raises a TimeoutException or something like that when a timeout happens?

issue_title: Variables & User Experience
issue_body:
I'm brand new here and evaluating stacker.  I love a lot of things already.

I have one behaviour I can't seem to grasp the intent of. 

I get that the `stack.yaml`  has variable definitions - and those map into the stacker classes `VARIABLES` dictionary.  
I also get that environment variables or cli overrides can replace template variables, similar to templating behaviour. 

Question: 

What is the common practice with parameters that are environment specific,  and as a template/stack author you want to declare as "must override".   The intent being that a "stack consumer" can't deploy your stack without specifying a parameter? 

In a lot of cases the author and consumer are the same person but I'm just using it to help with perspective on this question.

One example is  `VpcId`.   I probably want this to be environment specific, and don't want a default.

I could come up with a scheme like: 

stack.yaml
```
~ 
stacks:
   -name: Test
     variables:
         VpcId: 'notset'
```

template.py
```
~
 VARIABLES = {
        'VpcId': {'type': EC2VPCId, 'description': 'Vpc Id'},
```

Your output isn't very friendly.  It'll say something like `'notset' is not a valid VpcId`.

What do people do in this case? 

The reason I ask is if you put a variable in a template and someone comes by to run it - not realizing they should define their own environment file with their own VPC..  (maybe a bad usage example)..  but it might be hard to do. 

Other solutions might be 1) have something generate your stack.yaml (heavier lift).

I think I'm missing something in the documentation or examples.   If so - what am I missing?!

issue_title: Issue on sending content with specific length, NETCONF delimiter gets split
issue_body:
When trying to send (to a Juniper device) a set of commands with a specific length ncclient crashes.
It seems that the netconf delimiter (`]]>]]>`) is being split while transporting.

DEBUG level logging output:

```
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching message to <ncclient.operations.rpc.RPCReplyListener object at 0x7fde1eb66ad0>: <rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" xmlns:junos="http://xml.juniper.net/junos/15.1R5/junos" xmlns:nc="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="urn:uuid:ebf7759f-9a0d-4b90-a8ae-ac7818f68338">
DEBUG:ncclient.operations.rpc:Delivering to <ncclient.operations.third_party.juniper.rpc.ExecuteRpc object at 0x7fde25ab5e90>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:Trying another round of parsing since there is still data
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:parsing netconf v1.0
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:EOF in transport thread
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:Broke out of main loop, error=SessionCloseError('Unexpected session close\nIN_BUFFER: `\n`',)
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <ncclient.transport.session.NotificationHandler object at 0x7fde1eb66e10>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <jnpr.junos.device.DeviceSessionListener object at 0x7fde25f70d50>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <ncclient.operations.rpc.RPCReplyListener object at 0x7fde1eb66ad0>
```

While debugging, [this](https://github.com/ncclient/ncclient/blob/master/ncclient/transport/ssh.py#L135) seems to be the problematic code snippet:

```
        else:
            # handle case that MSG_DELIM is split over two chunks
            self._parsing_pos10 = buf.tell() - MSG_DELIM_LEN
            if self._parsing_pos10 < 0:
                self._parsing_pos10 = 0
```

This is located in `SSHSession._parse10()` which is called from `SSHSession.run()`. The latter reads message data and adds it into a buffer, for `_parse10()` to read. Then `_parse10()` tries to locate the 
NETCONF delimiter inside the buffer. If, however, the delimiter is split between two buffers it cannot locate it. I tried altering the code in `_parse10()` to move the buffer pointer in the end of the buffer, hoping that when `run()` loads the next chunk directly afterwards the delimiter will be complete and the
next execution of `_parse10()` will locate it correctly. I did not succeed though.

Any ideas?

To reproduce use the python code & input file provided in this [gist](https://gist.github.com/sergafts/fd0d036c2895e82ff66c005d19c4deca)

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash                                                                                                                                                                  

STORE_SOCKET_NAME="/tmp/store1"
RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME="/tmp/raylet1"

if [[ `stat $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME` ]]; then
  rm $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME
fi
if [[ `stat $STORE_SOCKET_NAME` ]]; then
  rm $STORE_SOCKET_NAME
fi

./src/plasma/plasma_store -m 1000000000 -s $STORE_SOCKET_NAME &
./src/ray/raylet/raylet $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME $STORE_SOCKET_NAME 172.31.20.117 172.31.23.99 6379 "" &

#!/usr/bin/env bash

STORE_SOCKET_NAME="/tmp/store1"
RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME="/tmp/raylet1"

if [[ `stat $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME` ]]; then
  rm $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME
fi
if [[ `stat $STORE_SOCKET_NAME` ]]; then
  rm $STORE_SOCKET_NAME
fi

./src/plasma/plasma_store -m 100000000000 -s $STORE_SOCKET_NAME &
./src/ray/raylet/raylet $RAYLET_SOCKET_NAME $STORE_SOCKET_NAME 172.31.23.99 172.31.23.99 6379 "" &
```

```js
var doc = yaml.safeLoadAll(yamlFileContents);
```

issue_title: Rename package

We made a mistake when initially naming this package with making it so EmberJS-centric.  

With a 2.0 release, let's include what is currently in master, plus renaming.  To maintain backwards compatibility and make things not too difficult, we can do a 1.6 release with what is in master as well.

issue_title: Throttling
issue_body: Since `JSONWebTokenAPIView` sets `throttle_classes = ()`, a global throttle won't actually apply to views that inherit from `JSONWebTokenAPIView`.  Wouldn't it be better to leave `throttle_classes = api_settings.DEFAULT_THROTTLE_CLASSES` as in the `APIView` it inherits from?

Related to #87 and #105

issue_title: [Proposal] Login using Auth0 JWT token in a django rest framework API
issue_body:
Hi colleagues!

I'd working on some modifications to make easy and fast to use Auth0 in any Django Rest Framework app using this library and i want to propose the following approach to do that:


- **Generate a new setting attribute to select the field name given to the username in the returned payload** this could be used to easily specify that without need to create(or copy) and configure a specific handler in each API we create. The current stable version of the library has

> def jwt_get_username_from_payload_handler(payload):
    """
    Override this function if username is formatted differently in payload
    """
    return payload.get('username')

and i want to change it to

> def jwt_get_username_from_payload_handler(payload):
    """
    Override this function if username is formatted differently in payload
    """
    return payload.get(api_settings.JWT_PAYLOAD_USERNAME_FIELD)

with default JWT_PAYLOAD_USERNAME_FIELD setted to 'username'


- **add a configuration field to specify if auth0 is being used, validate it and use the `base64.b64decode` function used by Auth0** to decode the Auth0 secret, the field would be something like `JWT_IS_AUTH0` with default to `False`

At the end of `rest_framework_jwt/settings.py` add the following code:
>if(api_settings.JWT_IS_AUTH0):
    api_settings.JWT_SECRET_KEY=base64.b64decode(api_settings.JWT_SECRET_KEY.replace("_","/").replace("-","+"))


-**Auto-Create users based on the username** instead of use of `get_by_natural_keys` to retrieve the user in the `authenticate_credentials` function from `rest_framework_jwt` use `get_or_create(username)` with the given username -`sub` field in Auth0 returned payload-. It's a sort of "stateless user" which allows us to simply asociate anything in our rest apis with the current user in the system. obviously i want to check if it works well with the current login scenarios to not to break anything

'The username in an Auth0 must be -for example- the `sub` attribute in the payload returned, so it's unique and let us to properly identify the current user'

The code i want to include is the following
>response = User.objects.get_or_create(username=username)
            created = response[1]
            user = response[0]
            if created:
                user.save()


I hope to hear from you soon. Any feedback would be apreciated

Grettings from Chile

issue_title: Difference between h5 file asc file and pneumatk
issue_body:
I was using pneumatk in the past, and now I am trying to transition to NeuroM, but it seems that there is a small difference between the results of pneumatk and NeuroM. Also there is a very big difference between NeuroM h5 file and asc file, I think those files should be identical:

```python

from neurom import ezy
from neurom.features import NEURITEFEATURES, NEURONFEATURES
from neurom.core.types import TreeType


H5pop = [ezy.load_neuron(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.h5']]
H5neurom = [] 
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_lengths'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_volumes'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_areas'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])

import pneumatk as pn
popPne = [ pn.io.load(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.h5']]
H5pnue =[]
H5pnue.append([sum(i.get_section_lengths(neurite_type='basal')) for i in popPne])
H5pnue.append([sum(i.get_section_volumes(neurite_type='basal')) for i in popPne])
H5pnue.append([sum(i.get_section_surface_areas(neurite_type='basal')) for i in popPne])

ASCpop = [ezy.load_neuron(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.asc']]
ASCneurom =[]
ASCneurom.append([sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_lengths'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)) for i in ASCpop])
ASCneurom.append([sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_volumes'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)) for i in ASCpop])
ASCneurom.append([sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_areas'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)) for i in ASCpop])


import pandas as pnd
pnd.DataFrame([H5pnue, H5neurom, ASCneurom],index = ['h5 pneumatk', 'h5 neurom', 'asc neurom'], columns = ['Basal length' , 'Basal volume', 'Basal area'] )

```


The code result in this output
```
                Basal length     Basal volume       Basal area
h5 pneumatk  [3155.24807618]  [1336.19777075]  [6218.93758029]
h5 neurom          [3109.97]        [1345.05]        [6703.85]
asc neurom   [3109.96568975]  [5380.19536895]  [13624.3941565]
```
The mrphologies are in this file
[Morphs.zip](https://github.com/BlueBrain/NeuroM/files/247541/Morphs.zip)
The morphologies were taken from 
l2/2012.07.23/morphologies/ascii
and 
l2/2012.07.23/morphologies/h5

What do you think?
Thanks.

issue_title: Adopt unified feature calculation output format (numpy arrays)
issue_body:
In order to avoid complex handling of the results of the feature functions I would like to suggest that we adopt a "duck" format for the output of the latter even for functions such as "get_n_segments".

For example, if all the outputs were numpy arrays (bonus if the are flattened as well) then a special handling would not be necessary and the stat extraction would apply the same for any feature function.

Do you guys think that this would cause any kind of conflict?

issue_title: Cannot build APK with python3crystax and flask - conflicting dependencies
issue_body:
@kchomski commented on [Wed Mar 01 2017](https://github.com/kivy/buildozer/issues/477)

Hi,

It's not possible to build APK when `python3crystax` and `flask` are both in requirements (or maybe I am doing something terribly wrong).
From what I can see the problem comes from conflicting dependencies in recipes: `flask` depends on (`python2` OR `python3crystax`) AND `setuptools`, but `setuptools` depends only on `python2`, which conflicts with `python3crystax`.

```
[INFO]:    Will compile for the following archs: armeabi-v7a
[INFO]:    Found Android API target in $ANDROIDAPI
[INFO]:    Available Android APIs are (19)
[INFO]:    Requested API target 19 is available, continuing.
[INFO]:    Found NDK dir in $ANDROIDNDK
[INFO]:    Got NDK version from $ANDROIDNDKVER
[WARNING]: NDK version was set as r10.3.2, but checking the NDK dir claims it is 10.3.2.
[WARNING]: The build will try to continue, but it may fail and you should check that your setting is correct.
[WARNING]: If the NDK dir result is correct, you don't need to manually set the NDK ver.
[INFO]:    Using Crystax NDK r10.3.2
[INFO]:    Found virtualenv at /usr/local/bin/virtualenv
[INFO]:    Found the following toolchain versions: ['4.9', '5', 'clang3.6', 'clang3.7']
[INFO]:    Picking the latest gcc toolchain, here 5
[INFO]:    No existing dists meet the given requirements!
[INFO]:    No dist exists that meets your requirements, so one will be built.
[INFO]:    Bootstrap requires recipes ['genericndkbuild', ('python2', 'python3crystax')]
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe flask (depends on [('python2', 'python3crystax'), 'setuptools'])
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe genericndkbuild (depends on [('python2', 'python3crystax')], conflicts ['sdl2', 'pygame', 'sdl'])
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe python3crystax (depends on ['hostpython3crystax'], conflicts ['python2', 'python3'])
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe setuptools (depends on ['python2'])
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe hostpython3crystax (depends on [], conflicts ['hostpython2'])
[INFO]:    Loaded recipe python2 (depends on ['hostpython2'], conflicts ['python3crystax', 'python3'])
[WARNING]: python2 conflicts with python3crystax, but both have been included or pulled into the requirements.
[WARNING]: Due to this conflict the build cannot continue, exiting.
```

---

@tito commented on [Mon May 01 2017](https://github.com/kivy/buildozer/issues/477#issuecomment-298331456)

This is an issue with python-for-android itself. I'm moving it to the proper project.

issue_title: stripe.InvoiceItem.create() created error "unknown parameters: period, date"
issue_body:
I'm trying to create an invoiceitem for test purposes. Without `date` and `period` parameters it works fine, but adding one of these yields a `stripe.error.InvalidRequestError`.

Example:

```python
import stripe, pytz, time
from datetime import datetime

stripe.InvoiceItem.create(
    customer=customer.id,
    amount=1000,
    currency="eur",
    date=int(time.mktime(datetime(2016, 6, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, pytz.utc).timetuple())),
    description="Test item",
    discountable=True,
    metadata={"usage_id": "12345abcde"},
    period={
        "start": int(time.mktime(datetime(2016, 5, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, pytz.utc).timetuple())),

issue_title: Implementing quantiles for Summary: exact or approximate?
issue_body: @brian-brazil welcomed PRs implementing quantiles for summaries in #92. I have a simplistic implementation that calculates quantiles exactly, but this requires keeping around all observations within the sliding window. I noticed that all other clients use Cormode and Muthukrishnan's [approximate algorithm](http://www.cs.rutgers.edu/~muthu/bquant.pdf). There is a Python package on [pyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/quantile-python) that implements this algorithm. Would you prefer that it be used instead? I haven't used it myself, and it's marked in alpha status, but then again, so is the one used by the Ruby client.

issue_title: should_autorenew ignores symlinks
issue_body:
Imagine the following scenario (which happened to a user). You've successfully completed your challenges from Let's Encrypt and you're renewing your certificates. After saving all four of `cert.pem`, `privkey.pem`, `chain.pem`, and `fullchain.pem` to `/etc/letsencrypt/archive`, you hit ctrl+C. Your symlinks in `/etc/letsencrypt/live` were never updated.

When you run `certbot renew` again, Certbot will tell your certificate is not due for renewal. This is because we currently check the expiration of the newest entry in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive` that has all four of the files listed above ([source](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/master/certbot/storage.py#L706)). We do not check the symlink.

There are a number of ways to fix this, but this is a pretty bad bug and should be resolved before we release 0.9.0.

issue_title: `bin/unpickle.py` shadows modules
issue_body:
If dill is installed (with pip, conda, or plain `setup.py install`) into a Python environment, the `bin/unpickle.py` can shadow a Python module. This doesn't occur interactively, but does occur if I run another script from the `bin` directory, as Python adds the scripts dirname (in this case, the environment's `bin`) to the front of `sys.path`.

For example, suppose I do,

```
$ conda create -n test python=2.7 pytest dill
$ source activate test
$ mkdir myproject
$ cat > myproject/unpickle.py
data = "Hello, world!"
$ export PYTHONPATH=$(pwd)/myproject
$ python -c 'from unpickle import data'
```

So far so good. But if I'm running Python via some script in `bin`, for example `bin/py.test`,

```
$ mkdir myproject/tests
$ cat > myproject/tests/test_unpickle.py
from unpickle import data  # same import as interactive session
$ py.test myproject/tests/test_unpickle.py 
============================= test session starts ==============================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.12, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/alex/tmp/dill, inifile: 
collected 0 items / 1 errors 

==================================== ERRORS ====================================
______________ ERROR collecting myproject/tests/test_unpickle.py _______________
ImportError while importing test module '/Users/alex/tmp/dill/myproject/tests/test_unpickle.py'.
Original error message:
'cannot import name data'
Make sure your test modules/packages have valid Python names.
!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Interrupted: 1 errors during collection !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
=========================== 1 error in 0.17 seconds ============================
```

This fails because under `.../bin/py.test`, the `bin` directory is in my path and dill's `unpickle.py` gets imported instead of mine.

In general, seems like a bad idea to install anything with a `.py` extension into `bin`, for this reason. Would it be possible to rename `unpickle.py` to just `unpickle`, or maybe `dill-unpickle`? Likewise for `get_objgraph.py`, though this seems less likely to collide with someone else's module.

issue_title: inconsistent results when infering
issue_body:
I trained a model with VGG16 on a dataset with accuracy of 96% on the validation set. However when I infer with the model I trained on the same validation set image by image and calculate the accuracy, I got 87%, which is quite smaller than 96%.

You know when training we make the dataset in .rec format, and for inference we read the image with cv2.imread().

What might be the reasons that I cannot get the same accuracy? All images have been resized to (224, 224) and subtracted by the mean.

FYI:
```
96%: mod.forward(batch, is_train=False), batch is from ImageRecordIter
87%: prob = mod.forward(Batch([mx.nd.array(img)])), img is from cv2.imread()
```

image preprocessing to get 96%
```
    data = mx.io.ImageRecordIter(
        data_name          = 'data',

```

image preprocessing to get 87%
```
def get_image(file_name):
    img = cv2.cvtColor(cv2.imread(file_name), cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
    if img is None:
         return None

    # convert into format (batch, RGB, width, height)
    img = cv2.resize(img, (224, 224))
    img = np.swapaxes(img, 0, 2)
    img = np.swapaxes(img, 1, 2)
    img = img.astype(np.float32)
    img -= mean_img
    img = img[np.newaxis, :]
    return img

```

Thanks.

issue_title: Python CustomOp limited to default type float32
issue_body:
This issue is related to #2300, which fixes it for SimpleOp.

I need to implement an operator via CustomOp, whose arguments (input, output) all have the same type, but this is not np.float32. In fact, I need np.float64 for numerical reasons.

The default implementation of InferType in include/mxnet/operator.h requires all types to be float32.
This is fixed in src/operator/operator_util.cc, where InferType now does the sensible thing, namely requires that all types are the same, or unspecified.

But this does not extend to CustomOp (Python API).

I tried to insert this code into src/operator/custom-inl.h. With this, simple_bind works without exception, but the kernel crashes when any computation is done. So there seems to be a "deeper" reason for the type to be float32.

What I find funny is that casting things from float32 to float64 works totally fine when I build my operator using mxnet.symbol only. It is just once I use CustomOp somewhere that things break. This means that float64 is supported in principle, but not everywhere.

Thanks for some help.

issue_title: define a new Parametrized symbol layer and  how to use (bind, init, set learning rate ) it?
issue_body:
I have been learning to define Parametrized layer, of which parameters will be leared during training time. I start by defining a fc layer with reference to
